note: Mathlib/Geometry/Euclidean/Triangle.lean:284-288, docstring '**Isosceles Triangle Theorem**: Pons asinorum': dist p1 p2 = dist p1 p3 -> angle p1 p2 p3 = angle p1 p3 p2. The classic named theorem about isosceles triangles, not a definition of the object; Mathlib has no IsIsosceles predicate (grep confirmed no other hits).
verify_note: Decl confirmed in cache+source at Triangle.lean:284-286, docstring literally says 'Isosceles Triangle Theorem: Pons asinorum'; repo-wide grep for Isosceles/IsIsosceles turns up only this one hit, so 'related' (not 'exact', since there's no IsIsosceles predicate to be exact to) is the honest, non-overclaiming label; QID label/description on Wikidata matches 'isosceles triangle'.

note: Mathlib/Algebra/Group/Even.lean:57 def IsSquare (a : alpha) := exists r, a = r*r (general Mul type). Mathlib/NumberTheory/LegendreSymbol/{Basic,QuadraticReciprocity}.lean use IsSquare (a : ZMod p) throughout (e.g. Basic.lean:62 euler_criterion, Basic.lean:180 eq_one_iff) as the standard formalization of 'a is a quadratic residue mod p'; no ZMod-specific QuadraticResidue predicate exists.
verify_note: Decl confirmed in cache+source at Algebra/Group/Even.lean:53-54 (general Mul-type def); LegendreSymbol/Basic.lean euler_criterion/eq_one_iff/eq_neg_one_iff confirmed to use IsSquare (a : ZMod p) exactly as claimed; repo-wide grep for QuadraticResidue/quadraticResidue/IsQuadraticResidue is empty, so IsSquare is genuinely the specialization point and 'generalization' (general Mul type vs. the ZMod-p-specific concept) is the honest label, not 'exact'; QID label/description matches 'quadratic residue'.
